exam.program: Anthropic Claude Partner Network / Anthropic Academy

scoring_model.note: Anthropic's official scaling is proprietary and non-linear. This trainer uses a documented linear approximation so the 720 pass line maps to ~69% correct, consistent with the real exam's reported difficulty.

domains.covers: Agentic loops, workflows vs. agents, prompt-chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-worker, evaluator-optimizer, single- vs. multi-agent trade-offs, subagents and context isolation, session/state management, human-in-the-loop, the Agent SDK harness.

name.fr: Configuration et workflows de Claude Code

domains.covers: CLAUDE.md memory hierarchy and imports, settings.json precedence and permissions, custom slash commands, hooks, project subagents, MCP configuration, headless/CI mode, plan mode, the Explore-Plan-Code-Commit workflow, GitHub Actions integration.

name.fr: Ingénierie des prompts et sorties structurées

domains.covers: Clear and direct instructions, multishot examples, chain-of-thought, XML tag structuring, system prompts, prefilling, output-format control, reliable JSON via tool use, temperature and stop sequences, prompt templates, evaluations and grading, hallucination mitigation.

Tool definitions and input schemas, writing good tool descriptions, the tool_use/tool_result flow, parallel tool calls, forcing tool use, token-efficient tools, MCP architecture (host/client/server), MCP primitives (tools/resources/prompts), transports, the MCP connector, tool error handling and security.

Context-window budgeting, prompt caching (cache_control, breakpoints, TTL, prefix ordering), context editing and tool-result clearing, compaction/summarization, agentic memory, retries and exponential backoff, idempotency, rate limits and 429/529 handling, streaming, cost optimization.

session.sampling: Without replacement within a session; per-domain counts follow session_count (16/12/12/11/9 = 60), mirroring the real domain distribution.

pool.rounding_rule: Pool: weight x 300, exact integers (81/60/60/54/45). Session: weight x 60 with largest-remainder rounding (16/12/12/11/9); the 0.8 remainder on Tool Design rounds up to keep the total at 60.

sources_note: Exam mechanics and domain weights were confirmed against first-party Anthropic announcements and the public CCA-F description in June 2026. See resources/README.md for the full source list and access dates.
